summary.intent: Refactor tests in test_release_from_fbc.py to stop duplicating validation logic and instead invoke the actual pipeline runtime validation and CLI command paths.
summary.outcome: Successfully refactored TestCliValidation tests (lines 583-614) to use CliRunner against the real release_from_fbc Click command. All 44 tests pass and formatting is clean.

repo: The release_from_fbc command uses @pass_runtime decorator requiring a Runtime object in Click context and @click_coroutine for async handling
CLI validation logic lives at lines 1047-1051 in the release_from_fbc command, checking if both fbc-pullspecs and extra-image-nvrs are empty
The cli group is imported from pyartcd.cli and release_from_fbc is registered as a subcommand

code.finding: Tests duplicated validation logic instead of invoking pipeline.run() to exercise actual code paths
CLI validation tests duplicated parsing logic instead of using CliRunner to invoke the real Click command

workflow: Using CliRunner with @pass_runtime requires MagicMock(spec=Runtime) to pass isinstance checks in Click's context finding
Async tests can consume the event loop causing later tests to fail with 'no current event loop' errors
@click_coroutine decorator calls asyncio.get_event_loop() before function body executes, requiring event loop setup even for tests that should fail early
Tests should exercise actual code paths rather than duplicating implementation logic to avoid false positives on regressions

friction: MagicMock() alone didn't satisfy isinstance check for Runtime - needed MagicMock(spec=Runtime)
Event loop consumed by earlier async tests caused failures when running full test suite
@click_coroutine decorator complexity required event loop setup even for validation tests that should fail before async code runs
open_items: Commit and push without --no-verify flag as requested by user
